description: /mathlib-quality:mathlibable over-produces `YES-but-generalise-first` verdicts. It emits that verdict when a declaration merely *looks* over-specialized (stated over a type variable, or carrying hypotheses/typeclasses that seem droppable) WITHOUT verifying a strictly-more-general form is actually provable and mechanically reachable. At scale this floods lane:generalise with un-actionable tickets that loop forever. FIX: gate the Phase 7 `YES-but-generalise-first` verdict on POSITIVE evidence — name the SPECIFIC hypothesis/typeclass to drop AND show the proof does not essentially use it (the Phase-4 mechanical-weakening attempt must actually succeed; 'literature standard is more general' or 'looks specialized' is NOT sufficient). Route to the correct bucket instead when: (1) ALREADY MAXIMALLY GENERAL — every hypothesis is used, e.g. `[CommRing R]` with subtraction in the proof is the floor (no CommSemiring weakening) -> already-general / NO-composable, not generalise-first; (2) CONCRETE, NO TYPE VARIABLE — a specific object like ℤ_[p]; 'generalising' = re-developing machinery, not weakening -> not generalise-first; (3) ALL INSTANCES USED — e.g. [CompleteSpace L] needed for series convergence -> nothing to drop; (4) GENERAL FORM ALREADY IN MATHLIB — the decl is a specialization of an existing mathlib lemma -> NO-mathlib-has-it; (5) REAL BUT NOT A SINGLE MECHANICAL EDIT — foundational def threaded through a development, or a multi-file refactor -> BORDERLINE-needs-human / dev-ticket. Evidence at scale: the EDS cluster (~13 lemmas) were all bucket 1; ℤ_[p] padicExp/extLog lemmas were buckets 2/3; isUnit_two_padicInt was bucket 4 (n=2 of PadicInt.isUnit_natCast_of_not_dvd); primeIdealZetaSum was bucket 5.
